glasswing_cves.notes: VulnCheck analysis still pins attributable CVE count at 40. Only CVE-2026-4747 is explicitly named as a Glasswing/Mythos autonomous discovery; the others are inferred from researcher affiliation. Anthropic has signaled a fuller disclosure around July 2026.

ms_patch_tuesday.source: Tenable blog (163); variance across sources: 163–168 depending on Edge/Chromium inclusion

rhel8_errata.source: Estimated / prior-run value retained (Red Hat does not publish clean monthly aggregate; stack.watch RHEL8 page returned 404 on this run)

projection_comparison.commentary: April 2026 is extrapolating to ~6242 — slightly BELOW the pre-launch conservative anchor of 6500 and only marginally above March actual of 6304. This is consistent with the counter-argument that NVD publishing latency (typically 4–8 weeks from discovery to publish) means Glasswing-attributable CVEs have not yet shown up in the April feed. Expect the first meaningful Glasswing signal in May–June 2026, with fuller visibility after Anthropic's planned July disclosure.

counter_arguments: CVE volume has NOT spiked post-Glasswing launch (April 7) in a meaningful way through day 17: April is pacing ~roughly flat to March, not the +500 to +5000/mo scenarios. Two reads: (a) NVD publishing latency is 4–8 weeks — Glasswing discoveries may not appear yet; (b) Glasswing disclosures are being staged through coordinated vendor patches, so they'll arrive in a lumpier distribution than monthly volume suggests.
The March 2026 spike to 6,304 (+57% YoY vs March 2025) clearly predates Glasswing and is driven by CNA expansion, automated fuzzing, and broader disclosure norms — not AI discovery.

CISA KEV April additions (14 through day 17) are on pace with the 2026 monthly average (~24/mo) — no AI-driven KEV inflection visible yet. And KEV additions are editorial decisions by CISA, not purely volume-driven.
Microsoft's 163-CVE April Patch Tuesday is notable, but the October 2025 spike of 167 occurred pre-Glasswing, so high-volume Patch Tuesdays are not novel.
